reasoning: France have been the tournament's standout side, winning all four matches and outscoring opponents 13-2, and both bookmakers and the Opta supercomputer now install Deschamps' deep, Mbappe-led squad as clear favorites (around 18.7% and market-leading odds). Argentina are the biggest beneficiary of a soft bracket path (Egypt, then Colombia/Switzerland before a likely Brazil/England semi), but the 3-2 extra-time scare against Cape Verde exposed some fragility for the defending champions. Spain remain the most complete possession side yet face a brutal early Portugal test and a demanding route, while England and Brazil have looked capable but inconsistent. Morocco are the live outsider: having reached a second straight quarterfinal by knocking out the Netherlands and Canada, their organization and rising confidence make them dangerous, and shock exits by Germany and the Netherlands show this knockout stage is volatile.
